compare_patched_vs_baseline.explanation: Compare patched vs baseline is a button in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
compare_patched_vs_baseline.practical_use: Typical values: Current target prompt and active patch recipe. Use it to run a baseline and patched output for side-by-side comparison.
compare_patched_vs_baseline.title: Compare patched vs baseline
compare_patched_vs_baseline.warning_if_any: It performs two generations, so runtime and model variance both increase.

direct_steering_direction.explanation: Direction is a dropdown in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
direct_steering_direction.practical_use: Typical values: Toward or Away. Use Toward for positive steering and Away for negative steering.
direct_steering_direction.title: Direction
direct_steering_direction.warning_if_any: Away sends a negative strength, so verify direction before running.

direct_steering_enabled.explanation: Enable Direct Steering is a toggle in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
direct_steering_enabled.practical_use: Typical values: Off until targets validate and the backend advertises support. Use it to include direct activation steering in the next local chat request.
direct_steering_enabled.title: Enable Direct Steering
direct_steering_enabled.warning_if_any: Unsupported backends reject the request rather than falling back.

direct_steering_strength.explanation: Strength is a slider in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
direct_steering_strength.practical_use: Typical values: 0.0 to 2.0; default 0.4. Increase it for stronger direct activation steering or reduce it for subtler changes.
direct_steering_strength.title: Strength
direct_steering_strength.warning_if_any: High strength can degrade coherence or overpower the base model.

direct_steering_targets.explanation: Target Node IDs is a text area in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
direct_steering_targets.practical_use: Typical values: L36-N175, L40-N18, or one ID per line. Use it to choose Activation Map nodes as steering targets.
direct_steering_targets.title: Target Node IDs
direct_steering_targets.warning_if_any: Malformed IDs are rejected before a request is sent.

direct_steering_token_scope.explanation: Token Scope is a dropdown in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
direct_steering_token_scope.practical_use: Typical values: All tokens, Prompt tokens, or Generated tokens. Use it to choose where the direct activation steering applies.
direct_steering_token_scope.title: Token Scope
direct_steering_token_scope.warning_if_any: A scope that misses the relevant tokens may produce little visible change.

patch_layer.explanation: Patch layer is a dropdown in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_layer.practical_use: Typical values: Any layer captured in the source trace. Use it to choose which layer's activation slice the patch modifies.
patch_layer.title: Patch layer
patch_layer.warning_if_any: Layer effects vary by model; a layer that works for one behavior may do little for another.

patch_mode.explanation: Patch mode is a dropdown in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_mode.practical_use: Typical values: blend, replace, add_delta, scale, or zero. Use it to choose how source activations modify target activations.
patch_mode.title: Patch mode
patch_mode.warning_if_any: Replace and zero are strong interventions and can produce brittle outputs.

patch_node_end.explanation: Node/channel end is a text input in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_node_end.practical_use: Typical values: Blank for all channels, or an index after the start. Use it to close the channel range for a targeted patch.
patch_node_end.title: Node/channel end
patch_node_end.warning_if_any: Invalid ranges are rejected and overly wide ranges can obscure causality.

patch_node_start.explanation: Node/channel start is a text input in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_node_start.practical_use: Typical values: Blank for all channels, or a zero-based channel index. Use it to limit the patch to a specific activation channel range.
patch_node_start.title: Node/channel start
patch_node_start.warning_if_any: Narrow ranges are easier to inspect but may miss distributed behavior.

patch_recipe_load.explanation: Load recipe is a dropdown in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_recipe_load.practical_use: Typical values: Any saved activation patch recipe. Use it to activate a saved patch recipe for the next patched generation.
patch_recipe_load.title: Load recipe
patch_recipe_load.warning_if_any: A recipe built from old runs may not match the current source artifact.

patch_recipe_name.explanation: Recipe name is a text input in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_recipe_name.practical_use: Typical values: Names that include source run and layer. Use it to name the saved activation patch recipe.
patch_recipe_name.title: Recipe name
patch_recipe_name.warning_if_any: Reusing a name can replace an earlier recipe.

patch_source_run.explanation: Source run is a dropdown in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_source_run.practical_use: Typical values: A saved single-message or batch run with trace rows. Use it to choose where activation values are copied or transformed from.
patch_source_run.title: Source run
patch_source_run.warning_if_any: Runs without captured activations cannot produce useful patch targets.

patch_strength.explanation: Patch strength is a slider in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_strength.practical_use: Typical values: 0.0 to 2.0; default 0.35. Use it to scale the patch effect for blend, delta, and scale modes.
patch_strength.title: Patch strength
patch_strength.warning_if_any: Large values can destabilize generation or make comparisons less interpretable.

patch_target_prompt.explanation: Target prompt is a text area in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_target_prompt.practical_use: Typical values: The prompt to run with the patch. Use it to edit the generation prompt for patched and comparison runs.
patch_target_prompt.title: Target prompt
patch_target_prompt.warning_if_any: Changing it after choosing runs can make the baseline comparison less direct.

patch_target_run.explanation: Target/current run is a dropdown in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_target_run.practical_use: Typical values: A saved run you want to compare against or patch. Use it to provide the target prompt and baseline context for patch comparison.
patch_target_run.title: Target/current run
patch_target_run.warning_if_any: Using an unrelated target run can make comparisons misleading.

patch_token_end.explanation: Token end is a number input in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_token_end.practical_use: Typical values: A token index at or after Token start. Use it to choose the final token in the activation patch span.
patch_token_end.title: Token end
patch_token_end.warning_if_any: Large spans can make the intervention harder to attribute.

patch_token_start.explanation: Token start is a number input in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
patch_token_start.practical_use: Typical values: Zero-based token index. Use it to choose the first target token in the activation patch span.
patch_token_start.title: Token start
patch_token_start.warning_if_any: Choosing the wrong token span can patch irrelevant context.

run_patched_generation.explanation: Run patched generation is a button in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
run_patched_generation.practical_use: Typical values: Current target prompt and active patch recipe. Use it to send one patched request to the local llama.cpp server.
run_patched_generation.title: Run patched generation
run_patched_generation.warning_if_any: It requires server-side activation patch support.

save_patch_recipe.explanation: Save patch recipe is a button in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
save_patch_recipe.practical_use: Typical values: The currently valid patch settings. Use it to store the patch recipe under data/activation_patch_recipes.
save_patch_recipe.title: Save patch recipe
save_patch_recipe.warning_if_any: Invalid recipes cannot be saved and saved recipes do not include source trace files.

steer_tab_clear.explanation: Clear Steer state is a button in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
steer_tab_clear.practical_use: Typical values: Built-in Steer defaults. Use it to reset only the Steer tab controls to defaults.
steer_tab_clear.title: Clear Steer state
steer_tab_clear.warning_if_any: It does not delete saved tab states, but unsaved Steer edits are discarded.

steer_tab_load.explanation: Load Steer state is a button in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
steer_tab_load.practical_use: Typical values: Any saved Steer tab state. Use it to restore only Steer controls from the selected tab state.
steer_tab_load.title: Load Steer state
steer_tab_load.warning_if_any: Other tabs and the global workspace are not changed.

steer_tab_load_name.explanation: Load Steer state selector is a dropdown in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
steer_tab_load_name.practical_use: Typical values: Any saved Steer tab state. Use it before pressing Load to choose which local Steer settings to restore.
steer_tab_load_name.title: Load Steer state selector
steer_tab_load_name.warning_if_any: Loading replaces only Steer tab settings, so unsaved Steer edits are lost.

steer_tab_save.explanation: Save Steer state is a button in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
steer_tab_save.practical_use: Typical values: Current Steer tab state name. Use it to overwrite the selected Steer tab state with the current controls.
steer_tab_save.title: Save Steer state
steer_tab_save.warning_if_any: It overwrites that tab state; use Save As when you need a new copy.

steer_tab_save_as.explanation: Save Steer state as is a button in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
steer_tab_save_as.practical_use: Typical values: A short local name. Use it to create a separate saved Steer tab state.
steer_tab_save_as.title: Save Steer state as
steer_tab_save_as.warning_if_any: Reusing a name replaces the existing saved Steer state with that name.

steer_tab_save_as_name.explanation: Steer Save As name is a text input in the Steer workflow. Changing it updates how local steering or activation patching is configured.
steer_tab_save_as_name.practical_use: Typical values: Names such as baseline_steer or high_strength_test. Use it to name the next Steer Save As target.
steer_tab_save_as_name.title: Steer Save As name
steer_tab_save_as_name.warning_if_any: Names are normalized for filesystem safety, so punctuation may become underscores.
